notes:
Runs perfectly out of the box with proton, no tweaks required (compared to wine requiring a few).

Minor issue: Changing resolution in the launcher from 1920x1080 (native) to 1600x900, results in unresponsive black screen upon launching the game, forcing a reboot. Changed resolution in the launcher back to native and game runs fine.

notes: The game runs perfectly, no issues. However on the MATE desktop environment middle mouse emulation is enabled by default which means you can't attack with both weapons while dual wielding. This can be fixed using xinput or with the command: "gsettings set org.mate.peripherals-mouse middle-button-enabled false"

notes: Had audio issue where after playing for a while I would get a lot of static (using pulseaudio). I installed the LADSPA Compressor plugin for PA and have had no issues since (https://github.com/gotbletu/shownotes/blob/master/pulseaudio-dynamic-range-compression.md). With that tweak it runs flawlessly for me, including all DLC and mods.

notes: Would be Platinum (ie., totally perfect) if the 'hang on exit' bug wasn't so prevalent.  Alt-tabbing to a waiting task manager isn't the way to shut down a 'platinum' game.  Other than that, performance is great, almost as good as playing on Windows with the same hardware.  SKSE and all game mods I've used work well.  (I do not use ENBs.)

notes: Upgraded the old XP machine to Linux, running 10%on OC.  Skyrim ran perfectly after installation. It is now running 85 mods, SKSE, SKUI, DLCs, Bruma, Maids II, Bijins, etc. Mod Organizer II works with Linux although  have to manually copy the files over to the Data folder.  LOOT doesn't work, I am running the same mods as my windows machine, so I just manually arrange the mods. TES5Edit also works so I can clean the mods and create the merged patch.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: I was running the game (vanilla with only a patch for my ultrawide aspect ratio) on the highest settings with very little issues in terms of performance. The only complaint I have is that it hangs on exit. To resolve this it's as easy as fetching the pid and killing it. However, I have slightly automated the process of killing the process with this tool I made that I call pill (repo: https://github.com/Aliics/pill). Just run "pill TESV" and she'll be gone.

notes: Everything works as expected. Have Hi-Res Texture Pack, Dawnguard, Dragonborn, Hearthfire and SSE High Quality Voices loaded. The only issue I have is can't quit to desktop as computer becomes unresponsive (cursor moves but doesn't respond to clicks or keyboard). Didn't deduct from rating because it doesn't affect play; quitting to Main Menu works and then I use System Monitor to kill the TESV process. Used to have some issues with sound or play becoming corrupted after long sessions (quitting & restarting solved them) but they seem to be gone with newer versions of Proton.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes:
Everything works, with performance almost on par with Windows.  However game hangs on exit.  If the system monitor running when you exit the game you can tab to it and send a kill request to the total stack of Steam TESV processes.

Mods also work if added manually.

Only other issue I have is quitting to desktop locks up the system so I quit to menu and then kill tesv process.

Mods must be sorted via original SkyrimLauncher.exe (not renamed skse_loader.exe)

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes:
My discrete gpu: AMD Radeon 520
I've encountered several problems:
1. Horizontal scrolling in map doesn't work (though you still can access any point on the map by rotating it and moving left/right)
2. Hangs on exit to desktop. The process in then marked as "<defunct>", it's easy to kill it with just "kill -9 $(ps | grep TESV | grep defunct | awk '{ print $2 })'
3. Rarely hangs on fast save
4. Had to put "DRI_PRIME=1 %command%" in launch options to make it use my discrete GPU.

But overall it's just excellent experience. I've never thought I could run someday my most favourite game on Linux. More than that, I'm running it from flatpak, so it means it will run in any distro.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Runs out of the box on Ubuntu MATE 18.04.3 LTS using D9VK. The game still hangs when exiting. Had to kill the game process through the task manager by switching to a different workspace (Ctrl + Alt + left or right arrow key) then accessing the task manager. Once the game hangs, the task manager cannot be accessed by pressing "Ctrl + Alt + Del". Must switch to a different workspace as mentioned.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Incredible performance with the latest 4.11-3. I was using 4.2-9 and experienced low FPS rates and CTDs often. I do use several mods, but nothing overwhelming. Move to 4.11-3 and at first experienced sound crackling and game stutter. I read one of the reviews here that said to use the following; [ "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=90 %command%" ] (taking off the first set of brackets and quotes) in the startup. This did the trick. Game play was smooth, and not a single CTD, sound issue or stutter. Unbelievable. Thank you!!
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design
notes: No issues encountered other than when exiting the game it is necessary to switch to a different workspace, open system monitor and kill TESV.exe from there. Otherwise runs great on Proton 4.11-3!
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Very Playable with high graphics audio bug fixes with the following command as others says: [ "WINEDLLOVERRIDES="xaudio2_7=n,b" PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=90 %command%" ]  30 fps + high graphics but sometimes fps drops
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes:
!! IMPORTANT: PLEASE READ!!
This report is for the security HARDENED kernel of ARCH linux. Something in that patch set makes Skyrim unplayable (it wont even start and crashes with an error related to memory management). HOWEVER, skyrim runs on the very same system and the same setup (e.g. in a flatpak) when using the DEFAULT kernel. So, if you're having troubles, consider using the default (albeit, more insecure) kernel config.

I also tested with various proton versions.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

I have not had any sound issues yet. Also used some mods with SKSE (remember to rename executables). Also skyrim must be killed after exiting the game.
